Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 197. On Fugitives (CT 53 839)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
oo destroyed

(Beginning destroyed)

Reverse
rr beginning broken away

r 1'1'

[ša] ⸢LUGAL⸣ EN iq-[bu-u-ni]

(1) [...... about whom] the king, my lord, sa[id]: "Let [......] with them and [...] the refugees!" —

r 2'2'

[ma]-a i-si-šú-nu lu [x x x]

r 3'3'

ma-a LÚ.ma-aq-tu [x x x]

r 4'4'

la i-ma-gu-ru ⸢la⸣ [x x x]

(4) they do not agree to [......]. When the men [......

r 5'5'

ki-ma LÚ.ERIM-⸢MEŠ⸣ [x x x x]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P314248/.
